name: Python
description: A high-level, general-purpose programming language known for its readability, simplicity, and extensive ecosystem. Powers web development, data science, AI/ML, automation, and more.
overview: Python, created by Guido van Rossum in 1991, is one of the world's most popular programming languages, prized for its clean syntax, readability, and 'batteries included' philosophy. It excels across multiple domains: web development (Django, Flask), data science (Pandas, NumPy), machine learning (TensorFlow, PyTorch), automation, scripting, and backend services. Python's dynamic typing, automatic memory management, and vast standard library make it ideal for rapid development. With the rise of AI/ML, Python has become the lingua franca of the data world. Its massive community contributes over 200,000 packages on PyPI, making virtually any functionality available via pip install.

learningPath.title: Python Fundamentals
learningPath.description: Master Python's core syntax, data types, control flow, and basic programming constructs. Build a strong foundation in Python programming.
topics: Python history, philosophy (Zen of Python) and use cases
Setting up Python (pyenv, conda, virtual environments)
IDEs and editors (VS Code, PyCharm, Jupyter Notebook)
Variables, dynamic typing, and naming conventions (PEP 8)
Basic data types (int, float, str, bool, None)
Control flow: if, elif, else, match-case (Python 3.10+)
Loops: for, while, break, continue, else clause on loops
Lists, tuples, and basic list operations

learningPath.title: Data Structures & Functions
learningPath.description: Deep dive into Python's built-in data structures, function definitions, scoping, and comprehensions.
topics: Lists in depth (methods, slicing, nested lists, copying)
Lambda functions and functional tools (map, filter, reduce)
Variable scope (LEGB rule: Local, Enclosing, Global, Built-in)
Documentation with docstrings and type hints (PEP 484)
projects: Custom CLI todo app with persistence

learningPath.title: Object-Oriented Programming in Python
learningPath.description: Learn Python's OOP model including classes, inheritance, polymorphism, magic methods, and advanced OOP features.
topics: Inheritance (single, multiple, MRO - Method Resolution Order)

learningPath.title: Modules, Packages & Standard Library
learningPath.description: Explore Python's module system, package management, and essential standard library modules for everyday tasks.
topics: os module (file paths, environment, process management)
json, csv, configparser for data serialization
argparse and click for CLI argument parsing
projects: CLI expense tracker with CSV persistence

learningPath.title: Error Handling, File I/O & Context Managers
learningPath.description: Master exception handling, file operations, serialization, context managers, and resource management in Python.
topics: File operations: open, read, write, append, binary modes
Context managers and the with statement

learningPath.title: Advanced Python: Iterators, Generators & Concurrency
learningPath.description: Master advanced Python concepts including iterators, generators, coroutines, threading, multiprocessing, and async/await.
topics: Thread synchronization (Lock, RLock, Semaphore, Event, Condition)
GIL (Global Interpreter Lock) and its implications
async context managers and async iterators

learningPath.title: Testing, Debugging & Profiling
learningPath.description: Learn Python testing frameworks, debugging techniques, profiling tools, and code quality practices.
topics: Debugging with pdb, ipdb, and IDE debuggers
Logging for debugging (logging module, log levels, handlers)
projects: Test suite for a library management app

learningPath.title: Web Development with Python
learningPath.description: Build web applications and REST APIs using Python's web frameworks including Django, Flask, and FastAPI.
topics: HTTP protocol fundamentals (methods, status codes, headers)
Django framework (models, views, templates, URLs, admin)
FastAPI (async endpoints, Pydantic models, OpenAPI docs)
REST API design principles and best practices
Form handling and validation (WTForms, Django forms)
WebSockets with Django Channels or FastAPI
projects: RESTful API with FastAPI for task management
E-commerce API with Django REST Framework

learningPath.title: Data Science & Visualization
learningPath.description: Learn Python's data science stack including NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Seaborn, and exploratory data analysis.
topics: NumPy arrays: creation, indexing, slicing, broadcasting
NumPy operations: linear algebra, statistics, random sampling
Data loading: read_csv, read_excel, read_sql, read_json
Data cleaning: handling missing values, duplicates, outliers
Data transformation: groupby, pivot, merge, join, concat
Time series data with Pandas (resample, rolling, shift)
Seaborn: statistical plots, themes, palettes, categorical plots
Working with large datasets (chunking, Dask basics)
projects: Stock market analysis with time series

learningPath.title: Advanced Python & Production Practices
learningPath.description: Master advanced Python topics including design patterns, performance optimization, packaging, deployment, and architecture.
topics: Design patterns in Python (Singleton, Factory, Strategy, Observer, Adapter)
C extensions with Cython and cffi
Performance optimization techniques (__slots__, caching, JIT with Numba)
Memory management and garbage collection in CPython
Creating and publishing packages to PyPI
CI/CD with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI
projects: Deploy a FastAPI app on Docker + AWS
Create and publish a pip package

interviewQuestions.question: What are Python decorators and how do they work?
interviewQuestions.answer: Decorators are functions that modify the behavior of other functions or methods. They use the @syntax and are essentially syntactic sugar for passing a function to a wrapper. A decorator takes a function as argument, defines an inner wrapper function, and returns the wrapper. They're commonly used for logging, access control, memoization, and timing.

interviewQuestions.question: Explain the difference between list, tuple, and set.
interviewQuestions.answer: List: mutable, ordered, allows duplicates, uses square brackets. Tuple: immutable, ordered, allows duplicates, uses parentheses, hashable (can be dict key). Set: mutable, unordered, no duplicates, uses curly braces, supports set operations (union, intersection). Lists are best for ordered sequences needing modification; tuples for fixed data like coordinates; sets for unique items and membership tests.

interviewQuestions.question: What is the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL)?
interviewQuestions.answer: GIL is a mutex in CPython that protects access to Python objects, preventing multiple threads from executing Python bytecode simultaneously. This simplifies memory management but limits CPU-bound parallelism. For I/O-bound tasks, threading still works. For CPU-bound tasks, use multiprocessing (separate processes with separate GILs), asyncio, or C extensions that release the GIL.

interviewQuestions.question: Explain generators vs iterators.
interviewQuestions.answer: Iterators are objects implementing __iter__ and __next__ methods. Generators are functions with yield that return generator objects (which are iterators). Generators maintain state between calls and produce values lazily, consuming less memory for large sequences. Generators are single-use (can't be reused). Generator expressions are like list comprehensions but with parentheses, producing values on-demand.

interviewQuestions.question: What are *args and **kwargs in Python?
interviewQuestions.answer: *args captures extra positional arguments as a tuple. **kwargs captures extra keyword arguments as a dictionary. They allow functions to accept variable-length arguments. Common in decorators, function wrappers, and when delegating to other functions. They can be combined with regular parameters but *args must come after regular params and before **kwargs.

interviewQuestions.question: How does Python's memory management work?
interviewQuestions.answer: Python uses reference counting (each object tracks how many references point to it, freed when count reaches 0) and a generational garbage collector for cyclic references. Objects are allocated on the heap. Small objects are managed by CPython's memory arena system. GC runs periodically on generation 0 (young), 1, and 2 objects. The gc module provides manual control. Circular references involving __del__ can cause memory leaks.

interviewQuestions.question: What are list comprehensions and when should you use them?
interviewQuestions.answer: List comprehensions provide a concise syntax for creating lists: [expression for item in iterable if condition]. They're faster than for loops in most cases (no append overhead). Use them for simple transformations and filtering. For complex logic or side effects, use regular loops. Dict and set comprehensions follow similar syntax. Generator expressions use parentheses instead of brackets.

interviewQuestions.question: Explain the difference between is and == in Python.
interviewQuestions.answer: == checks value equality (calls __eq__ method). is checks identity equality (id() — memory address). For small integers (-5 to 256) and small strings, Python caches and reuses objects so is may work coincidentally. Always use == for value comparison and is for None comparison (x is None) or checking singletons.

interviewQuestions.question: What is monkey patching in Python?
interviewQuestions.answer: Monkey patching is dynamically modifying classes or modules at runtime by replacing attributes, methods, or functions. Common in testing (replacing real implementations with mocks), adding functionality to third-party code, or fixing bugs without modifying source. Can lead to maintenance issues and unexpected behavior if overused. Frameworks like gevent use it to make standard library async.

interviewQuestions.question: How does async/await work in Python?
interviewQuestions.answer: async def defines a coroutine function that returns a coroutine object. await suspends execution until the awaited awaitable completes. The asyncio event loop manages scheduling of coroutines. Unlike threading, async uses cooperative multitasking (coroutines yield control explicitly at await points). Asyncio provides async I/O operations, synchronization primitives (Lock, Semaphore), and network transports without threads.

interviewQuestions.question: What are metaclasses in Python?
interviewQuestions.answer: Metaclasses are classes of classes that define how classes behave. type is the default metaclass. Metaclasses intercept class creation, allowing modification of class attributes, methods, or behavior before the class is created. Used in ORMs (SQLAlchemy, Django models), serialization frameworks, and for implementing singleton patterns. __metaclass__ or metaclass= keyword argument in class definition.

interviewQuestions.question: Explain the MRO (Method Resolution Order) in Python.
interviewQuestions.answer: MRO determines the order in which base classes are searched when looking up a method. Python uses C3 linearization (depth-first left-to-right but with monotonicity guarantees). Viewable via ClassName.__mro__. MRO ensures each class appears before its parents and respects the order in multiple inheritance. super() uses MRO to call the next class in the chain, not the parent class.

interviewQuestions.question: What is a context manager and how do you create one?
interviewQuestions.answer: Context managers handle resource setup and teardown using the with statement. Created with: __enter__ (run on entry) and __exit__ (run on exit, even on exception) methods; or using @contextmanager decorator from contextlib with yield separating setup/teardown. Common uses: file operations, database connections, locks, temporary changes. contextlib provides redirect_stdout, suppress, closing, and ExitStack.

interviewQuestions.question: How do you handle circular imports in Python?
interviewQuestions.answer: Circular imports occur when two modules import each other. Solutions: restructure code to avoid circular dependencies; move shared code to a common module; use lazy imports (import inside function/method instead of top-level); use importlib.import_module() dynamically; or use TYPE_CHECKING for type hints (with from __future__ import annotations). Design packages with clear dependency direction.

interviewQuestions.question: Explain the with statement and contextlib utilities.
interviewQuestions.answer: The with statement ensures proper acquisition and release of resources. contextlib provides: closing (call close on exit), suppress (ignore specified exceptions), redirect_stdout/redirect_stderr (temporarily redirect output), ExitStack (dynamic management of multiple context managers), nullcontext (no-op context manager). Custom context managers use @contextmanager decorator or implement __enter__/__exit__.

interviewQuestions.question: What are type hints and how do they improve Python code?
interviewQuestions.answer: Type hints (PEP 484) add optional type annotations to function signatures and variables. Tools: mypy for static type checking, pydantic for runtime validation, and IDE support for autocomplete. Benefits: early bug detection, self-documenting code, better IDE support, easier refactoring. Modern Python uses typing module (List, Dict, Optional, Union, Any, Callable, Protocol, TypeVar, Generic).

interviewQuestions.question: How do you optimize Python code performance?
interviewQuestions.answer: Profiling first (cProfile, timeit, py-spy). Common optimizations: use built-in functions (map, filter, reduce, any, all) over loops; use list comprehensions over for+append; prefer local variables over global; use __slots__ in classes; use collections.deque for pop(0); use generators for large sequences; use numpy for numerical operations; use functools.lru_cache for memoization; use Cython or Numba for hot paths; use PyPy as alternative interpreter.

interviewQuestions.question: What is the difference between deep and shallow copy?
interviewQuestions.answer: Shallow copy (copy.copy) creates a new object but inserts references to the original's child objects. Deep copy (copy.deepcopy) recursively creates copies of all nested objects. Shallow copy is faster but mutations of nested objects affect both. Deep copy is slower and may fail with circular references or non-copyable objects. Immutable types (int, str, tuple) don't need deep copying.

books.title: Python Crash Course
books.author: Eric Matthes
books.description: A hands-on, project-based introduction to Python covering fundamentals, game development, data visualization, and web applications.

books.title: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python
books.author: Al Sweigart
books.description: Practical programming for total beginners with real-world automation projects — web scraping, working with Excel/PDF/CSV, email automation, and more.

books.title: Fluent Python
books.author: Luciano Ramalho
books.description: Deep dive into Python's best features including data model, functions as objects, design patterns, metaprogramming, and concurrency models.

books.title: Effective Python
books.author: Brett Slatkin
books.description: 90 specific ways to write better Python covering functions, classes, concurrency, metaprogramming, productionization, and testing.

books.title: Python for Data Analysis

books.description: Comprehensive guide to data manipulation with Pandas, NumPy, and Jupyter by the creator of Pandas.

books.author: Mark Lutz
books.description: Comprehensive, in-depth tutorial covering all aspects of Python from basics to advanced topics like decorators, metaclasses, and C extensions.

books.title: Two Scoops of Django

books.description: Best practices for Django web development covering models, views, templates, APIs, security, and deployment.

books.title: Architecture Patterns with Python
books.author: Harry Percival
books.description: Learn DDD, CQRS, event-driven architecture, and repository patterns using Python with practical examples.

books.title: Test-Driven Development with Python

books.description: Comprehensive guide to web development with Django using TDD, covering unit tests, functional tests, and deployment.

books.title: Python Cookbook

books.title: High Performance Python
books.author: Micha Gorelick & Ian Ozsvald

projects.title: E-commerce Platform (Django)

projects.description: Build a pipeline that fetches from multiple APIs, processes data asynchronously, and stores results. Handle rate limiting and retries.

projects.title: Custom Python Interpreter
projects.description: Build a minimal Python-like language interpreter with lexer, parser, AST, and evaluator in pure Python.

bestPractices: Write code following PEP 8 style guide — use black and isort for automatic formatting
Use virtual environments (venv) for every project to isolate dependencies
Prefer list comprehensions and generator expressions over manual for loops for simple transformations
Use pathlib for all file path operations instead of os.path magic strings
Always use type hints for function signatures and class attributes
Use f-strings for string formatting (Python 3.6+) instead of % formatting or .format()
Prefer dataclasses for classes that primarily store data
Use context managers (with statements) for resource management (files, locks, connections)
Keep functions small and focused on a single responsibility
Use logging instead of print for production code with appropriate log levels
Write unit tests with pytest — aim for 80%+ coverage on business logic
Use early returns to reduce nesting and improve readability
Avoid mutable default arguments in function definitions
Use @property instead of getter/setter methods for computed attributes
Prefer composition over inheritance for flexible code reuse
Use exceptions for exceptional cases, not for regular control flow
Document public APIs with docstrings following Google/NumPy style
Use Enum for fixed sets of constants instead of string literals
Cache expensive function results with functools.lru_cache or @cache
Secure your code: validate inputs, use parameterized queries, never eval user input

careerOpportunities.description: Entry-level role focused on writing Python code, building scripts, fixing bugs, and learning the development workflow.
Design and build server-side applications, REST APIs, and microservices using Django, Flask, or FastAPI.
Analyze large datasets, build predictive models, create visualizations, and derive actionable business insights.
Build and maintain data pipelines, ETL processes, data warehouses, and big data infrastructure.
Design, train, deploy, and monitor ML models in production environments at scale.
Build automation scripts, CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure automation, and test automation frameworks.
Work across the entire stack — backend APIs, frontend integration, databases, and deployment.
Use Python for infrastructure automation, monitoring, configuration management, and platform engineering.
